chapter_title.en: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram

text: 2638 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَلَبِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «لاَ بَأْسَ أَنْ تُحْرِمَ اَلْمَرْأَةُ فِي اَلذَّهَبِ وَ اَلْخَزِّ وَ لَيْسَ يُكْرَهُ إِلاَّ اَلْحَرِيرُ اَلْمَحْضُ ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2638 - Al-Halabi narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"There is no harm if a woman enters Ihram wearing gold or khazz (a silk-blended fabric). What is disliked is only pure silk (al-harir al-mubham)."
